expectations.text: Response uses content-appropriate stage names (not generic code stages)
expectations.evidence: Lines 60-83: research/, script-lab/, production/, distribution/ — all content-specific stages

expectations.text: Response includes a dedicated voice or brand guidelines file with tone and audience sections
expectations.evidence: Lines 346-401: Detailed _config/voice.md with Tone, Phrases (use/avoid), Grammar, Opening Hook Structure, Explanation Style, Visual Language Notes

expectations.text: Response uses structured stage contracts with Inputs/Process/Outputs format
expectations.evidence: Lines 178-337: All four stage CONTEXT.md files with Inputs table (Source/File/Sections/Purpose), numbered Process steps, Outputs with file names and token ranges, Verify checklists

expectations.text: Response explains how brand voice stays persistent across stages
expectations.evidence: Lines 563-576: 'After ICM' section explicitly shows voice.md loaded as Input at every stage. 'Your voice is never forgotten. It is not buried in a large context. It is an explicit input at every stage.'

expectations.text: Response explains how output from one stage feeds into the next
expectations.evidence: Lines 146-166: Root CONTEXT.md with pipeline flow table and handoff rules. Line 157: 'Output of stage N becomes input of stage N+1'
